source: DT-2026-05-14-loop-build-validation-contracts; user approved all rows in chat on 2026-05-14

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Accepted feedback build should be validated from artifact only, not from chat memory.

next_loop.reason: Documentation loop should consume the accepted feedback build only after feedback gateway pass.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: CodeWiki must define and enforce central loop build schemas for feedback, documentation, planning, and implementation builds.
Compiler loops must remain active until an accepted build passes the relevant validation gateway; fail/block verdicts keep work in the same loop and supersede old attempts.
Compiler-exit validation reports must be strict gateway logs linked to exactly one source build with required verdict evidence.
Gateway contracts must define deterministic required evidence independent of whether the executor is user, agent, tool, or combination.
Feedback gateway pass requires row-level user decisions, final acceptance, relevant agent critique, and explicit open-question disposition.
Per-loop build and validation contracts must be represented in code and tested with small fixtures.
Fresh gateway sessions may continue as next compiler loop on pass only where independence remains safe; implementation/task-close/publish validation must stay independent.
Graph state must distinguish accepted, failed/blocked, validated-unconsumed, consumed, archived, and purgeable states.
Validation logs must be trashed with source builds through the same archive/purge lifecycle once restore context is preserved.
User-facing coordination should be presented as Session Queue, while existing claim/lease mechanics can remain internal or advanced compatibility detail.
The Pi adapter must provide an interactive row-level diff-table validation window before feedback build compilation.

agent_assessment: These decisions address a real policy/code drift: the backend needs enforceable schemas, deterministic gateway contracts, explicit validated-unconsumed graph state, and retention rules that trash validation logs with source builds once safe. The riskiest areas are over-strict contracts slowing small tasks, unsafe reuse of gateway sessions for implementation validation, and UI state becoming a second truth source. Best path is contract-first with TypeBox/runtime validation, small fixture tests, Pi-only row-level diff-table UX as adapter presentation, and compatibility aliases for existing claim mechanics.
summary: Accepted intent: establish explicit loop build and validation contracts, integrate gateway-driven session transitions, align validation-report GC with build purge lifecycle, rename user-facing coordination to Session Queue, and add Pi row-level diff-table approval window.

diff_table.current_state: Build contracts are split across TypeBox Pi tool schemas, ad-hoc writer checks, docs, skills, and graph heuristics.
diff_table.desired_state: Central build contract schemas for all loop builds; keep `accepted`; build is immutable loop attempt and semantic handoff artifact.
diff_table.rationale: Central schemas prevent policy prose drift; legacy partial builds may need versioning/backfill.
diff_table.risk: Legacy artifacts may not match strict schemas.

diff_table.current_state: Loop exit criteria are partly policy text and not backend-enforced.
diff_table.desired_state: Compiler loop exits only when accepted candidate build passes gateway; fail/block stays in-loop and next attempt supersedes old build.
diff_table.rationale: This is the exact drift observed; graph/GC must collapse superseded noise without hiding blockers.
diff_table.risk: Superseding attempts can clutter hot state.

diff_table.current_state: Validation reports are generic and source linkage can be loose.
diff_table.desired_state: Central validation report contract; compiler-exit reports reference exactly one source build and record profile, verdict, evidence, rationale, issues, failed criteria, blocking questions, and isolation/session evidence.
diff_table.rationale: Strict source links let graph route, audit, and trash reports with source builds; audits may need separate multi-source path.
diff_table.risk: Need separate contract for multi-source audits/release checks.

diff_table.current_state: Gateway, validator, and executor language is muddy.
diff_table.desired_state: Gateway contract is deterministic rules plus required evidence; executor may be user, agent, tool, or combination.
diff_table.rationale: Clarifies system, but rules must be concrete to avoid fake determinism.
diff_table.risk: Checklist theater if evidence is vague.

diff_table.current_state: Feedback gateway is not formalized as user approval plus mandatory agent critique.
diff_table.desired_state: Feedback pass requires row decisions, explicit final user acceptance, agent assessment, and answered/accepted/blocking open-question disposition.
diff_table.rationale: Prevents weak understanding from being laundered downstream; critique must be relevant, not generic.
diff_table.risk: Generic assessment could satisfy schema without real grounding.

diff_table.desired_state: Documentation maps feedback to KB; planning maps KB to tasks/acceptance/TDD/code-test candidates; implementation maps task/plan to tests/code/checks/acceptance evidence/risks/closure brief.
diff_table.rationale: Needed but must stay minimal and support explicit N/A for small changes.
diff_table.risk: Overstrict contracts can slow trivial work.

diff_table.current_state: Validation criteria are spread across docs and ad-hoc checks.
diff_table.desired_state: Per-loop validation contracts with risk-tiering; implementation/task-close/publish require fresh context, clean worktree, checked SHA when code/publication risk exists.
diff_table.rationale: Correct risk-tiering; planning approval must not be confused with implementation proof.
diff_table.risk: Boundary between planning and implementation proof must stay explicit.

diff_table.current_state: Session handoff is separate from gateway flow and not clearly tied to pass/fail result.
diff_table.desired_state: Fresh gateway session validates accepted build; on pass it may continue as next compiler loop where safe; fail/block returns to original loop; implementation/task-close/publish validation remains independent.
diff_table.rationale: Reduces session churn while preserving freshness; builder must not validate own code.
diff_table.risk: Unsafe role reuse if not explicitly blocked.

diff_table.current_state: Graph can collapse pass validation into completion before downstream consumption.
diff_table.desired_state: Graph distinguishes accepted, failed/blocked, validated-unconsumed, consumed, archived, purgeable; pass makes build eligible, downstream evidence marks consumed.
diff_table.rationale: Validation is not consumption; visible validated-unconsumed inputs preserve next action.
diff_table.risk: Needs good summarization to avoid clutter.

diff_table.current_state: Runtime handoff JSON can be mistaken for truth; `claim` is backend wording for user-visible coordination.
diff_table.desired_state: Runtime session handoff is launch envelope only; build is semantic handoff; user-facing coordination becomes Session Queue while internal lease/claim terms remain advanced/compatibility detail.
diff_table.rationale: Session Queue is better UX; alias/read model should precede broad migration.

diff_table.current_state: Graph already marks some pass validation reports purgeable with builds, but contract wording is unclear.
diff_table.desired_state: Validation gateway logs follow same hot/warm/cold/purgeable lifecycle as source builds and are trashed when source build is safely archived/purgeable with restore data preserved in git archive/ledger/digest.
diff_table.rationale: Reports should not have separate retention; preserve archive ledger, digests, push safety, source link, and restore path before deletion.
diff_table.risk: Premature deletion could destroy audit context.

diff_table.current_state: Tests do not cover full build→gateway→next-loop→consume→GC chain or Session Queue mapping.
diff_table.desired_state: Add contract tests for fail/block in-loop, pass validated-unconsumed, allowed gateway continuation, downstream consumption, minimal launch envelopes, report purge timing, and Session Queue mapping.
diff_table.rationale: Executable regression coverage needed to stop repeated policy/code drift; keep fixtures small.
diff_table.risk: Overlarge E2E tests can become brittle.

diff_table.current_state: Diff tables are emitted in chat/tool text; user validates whole table conversationally.
diff_table.desired_state: Pi adapter renders diff table in interactive window/modal; user validates each row individually by approve/edit/reject/defer; row decisions write through diff-table APIs; feedback build compiles accepted rows.
diff_table.rationale: Row-level validation prevents broad approval from hiding per-row disagreement; UI must not own semantic state.
diff_table.risk: UI-only state could create drift if not persisted through APIs.

accepted_decisions.summary: Keep `accepted` as lifecycle word for compiler-produced build attempts.
Build is the semantic handoff artifact; runtime handoff JSON is only launch envelope.
Compiler loop exits only after accepted build passes its gateway; fail/block remains in-loop with superseding attempts.
Gateway contracts must be deterministic evidence requirements separated from executor identity.
Validation reports are gateway logs and compiler-exit reports must point to exactly one source build.
Validation logs follow source-build GC lifecycle and are purgeable/trashable with safely archived source builds.
Fresh gateway session may become next compiler-loop session on pass for compiler-to-compiler transitions; implementation/task-close/publish validation remains independent.
Graph must distinguish validated-unconsumed from consumed.
User-facing coordination should be Session Queue, with claim/lease as internal compatibility detail.
Pi adapter should render feedback diff tables in an interactive row-level validation window first; other harnesses later via adapters.

non_goals: Do not implement cross-harness diff-table windows in this cycle.
Do not rename all internal claim files/tools in one breaking change.
Do not make runtime handoff JSON canonical truth.
Do not delete builds or validation logs without preserving restore context through existing archive ledger/digest/push-safety gates.

roadmap: Task(s) to enforce build/validation contracts, graph state transitions, GC alignment, Session Queue read model, and Pi diff-table approval window.
